entry.title: How to add an extension field in extension scenario
entry.content:
Customer has created an extension field and want to know how to extend it to another work center / sales document.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to add an extension field in extension scenario
entry.content:
Please follow below steps to extend the extension field:

 

  1. On the screen on which you created the extension field, enter adaptation mode by selecting Enter Adaptation Mode form the Adapt menu.
  2. Select Edit Screen from the Adapt menu.
  3. The adaptation panel opens on the right side of the screen.
  4. Under Extension Fields, select the extension field you created previously and scroll down until you can see the extension field details.
  5. Click the Further Usage link.
  6. On the Extension Scenarios tab of the Further Usage screen, you can see a list of all extension scenarios to which you can add this extension field.
  7. Click on the arrow icon next to the extension scenario name to view a list of data flows. Each data flow consists of a source and target business context. This reflects the direction in which data is passed from one business context to the next. An extension scenario can contain one or more data flows.
  8. Select the extension scenarios that you want to extend and click Add Field.
  9. The extension field is made available in all business contexts involved in the selected extension scenarios. This means that you can now add the extension field to all screens, form templates, data sources and reports that are based on these business contexts.
  10. To make the extension field visible on the screens that are part of each extension scenario, you must navigate to the screen, and select Enter Adaptation Mode from the Adapt menu.
  11. Select Edit Screen from the Adapt menu.
  12. Under Extension Fields, select the screen section on which you want the extension field to be visible and then select the Visible checkbox beside the extension field name.
  13. Save and publish your changes.

 

For more information please refer to the help document which provides brief explanation about extending the extension field https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2d8cdc29722d1014b876f1b250f32b7d.html

entry.title: SAP Fiori List Report - OData V4: List Not Refreshing automatically After Action (change)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

A few months ago, I built a Fiori List Report app for the SD module using OData V2 in an on-premise environment. The app had actions like "Remove Block," "Add Block," and "Add Comment," and it worked perfectly—after each action, the list report refreshed automatically and displayed the updated data.

Recently, I created a similar app as a Fiori List Report app, but this time using OData V4 in the same on-premise environment. Unfortunately, the actions no longer refresh the list report after submitting changes always I had to click "GO" in order to get it the list refreshed.

Steps Taken to Identify the Issue:

  1. Adjusted the UI5 Version to both newer and older versions.
  2. Implemented side effects using guided development tools.
  3. Compared the manifest.json, metadata.XML and other app components between the old (OData V2) and new (OData V4) apps and found significant differences.

Despite thorough testing and troubleshooting, I couldn’t resolve the issue.

Request:

Could someone guide me on how to ensure the list report refreshes properly after an action in a Fiori List Report app using OData V4?

Thank you in advance for your help!

Attached are the old and the new manifest

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Overview of tools for maintaining custom launchpad content and layou
entry.content:
Summary of my comment
I would like to be able to test any change in business catalogs on another client before releasing the transport. But I have difficulties with that.

Our situation
We have an environment where the DEV system is settled with two major clients (client 100 and client 110). Our procedure is to develop on the 100-client and to release the transports targeting the ACC system. When customization needs to be copied to client 110, we do that using the SCC1N. 

I am using tcode /n/ui2/flpcm_cust to assign a Tile and TM to a business catalog. This leads to a transport, containing a customizing task, containing objects of type 'customizing: table content'. 

When I release the transport, the content is available to be imported in the ACC env; But the content is not automatically transported to client 110. 

The challenge we have encountered is fed by the following limitations and restrictions: 

  • Using SCC1N we are not able to transport this content of a table. SCC1 does not copy the contents of a table - SAP Community
  • Using /n/ui2/FLPCM_CONF is not the recommended approach of changing custom business catalogs. SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Overview of tools for ... - SAP Community

That means, following the recommended approach, we have to: 

  • Release the transport
  • Import the transport into the client 110 using STMS

I am not feeling comfortable with that for the following two reasons: 

  • Releasing the transport leads in our case to an automatic import into our ACC-environment, while I have in mind that I want to have one business catalog for testing purpose sitting on the DEV environment. I prefer to have it in my case of today to be a local transport. Or, in more common cases, I want to be able to test it on the DEV client 110 before releasing it.
  • This also means that we have to implement a manual step in the organization after each change of business catalog. 

What I do not want as a solution. 

  • Our architect and internal IT supplier do not want to have the client 110 in the transport lane.
  • I do not want to develop directly on client 110 because we do all developments on 100 and I want our processes to be understandable and simple for newcomers and consultants by following the SAP standards as much as possible. 

What solution am I considering:

  • As a workaround, I am considering the implement BaDi CTS_REQUEST_CHECK to trigger a workflow when a transport is released carrying this specific FLPCM content. This workflow will raise a popup recommending importing the transport using STMS on client 110. Also this workflow will trigger a human action in the organization to validate if this action has been done. 

So Why do I submit this purposal to SAP. 
We have been using FLPD_CUST in the past and now I want us to use FLPCM_CUST. There are a lot of benefits; Like for example... this new tooling helps us to avoid making a mess. I am using FLPCM_CUST for a while now, and I feel very comfortable with it. I want to encourage my colleagues and future colleagues to use it to profit from all technical and functional benefits. But this missing piece that I have found, is very unfortunate. I would like to suggest the feature to be able to test any change in business catalogs on another client before releasing the transport. 

Questions and additional info: 
I have two questions. 

  • Am I missing something that might help me?
  • Would it be appreciated if I commit this suggestion to into SAP's Customer Influence and Adoption program, should I open a SAP Case, or is this not appropriated for this case (and if not, why not)? 

Additional info:

  • Our authorization consultant makes changes on the 100, but he and the whole team tests their own work on 110 before releasing it. This implies my case is differs from Micheals case.

Thanks in advance

Vladimir

entry.title: Re: controller and firefighter cannot be the same person
entry.content:
Hello Srinivasa, I guess it would be fine to get familiar with the four eyes principle because this is the approach and the purpose of SAP GRC (as the tool for Segregation of Duties). For SAP Security related SAP Role Management and User Management it's obviously clear that Role Administrator can create and change roles but can not be allowed to assign roles to users, and that User Administrator can create users but can not be allowed to give access rights to users, it's analogy of SOX but for IT.

SAP GRC is able to help to cover critical points of business related processes and also IT related processes. Even if usewd mainly, Emergency Access Management is only a part of SAP GRC complexity.

Have a nice day.

entry.content:
Hi

Just wondering if there is a way of selecting an historical date range in a story? You seem to only be able to select a range for so many days/months back to days/months forward. What if it’s November and I want to select a Date Completed range from September to October. Is that not possible?

I know stories are supposed to be a snapshot in time as of now but stories are supposed to be replacing canvas reports so stories should have some functionality similar to canvas. Because of the limit of 1 million cells in a story, you may have to select a small date range so you can export the data, and then select another small date range and export that. I know we are to discourage data export but some processes require it and if we can’t do it, it’s going to cause real issues.

Thanks

entry.title: Migrating from CR9 nowadays
entry.content:
Hello

I am looking for a solution to the following situation:

Quite a few years ago I developed a VB.NET project (Windows desktop application) using the Visual Studio 2005. As a reporting tool I used Crystal Reports 9. The project was designed to load the CR9 reports from an external folder and worked very well.
A year ago, I migrated succesfully this project using the Visual Studio 2022 without changing the way CR9 are loaded. In order to make the old reports run, I found in the Internet the CRRedist2005_X64.msi and CRRedist2005_X86.msi. After running them in the target machine the old reports displayed normally.
This is the story up to now. I know what you may think: this is not the normal developer's way in the year 2024. OK I agree.
Well here is my real question as I do not want:
a) to import and embed these reports into the VB.NET 2022 application for various reasons
b) neither I want to redesign them as they are many

According to your experience what would be the best way and route to go out of this situation?
Thanks a lot
Panagiotis Adam

entry.title: Re: Transfer parameters of a custom operation or custom hook in SAP Landscape Management (LaMa)
entry.content:
Hi,

I have some secure custom properties defined in LaMa under "Configuration Extensions -> Custom Properties" that I want to pass to my scripts.

Checking with the documentation I should use the LVM_SEC_PARAMS and retrieve the custom property I want from it in my script. This is my conf file line to pass it:

Command: <path of the script> $[LVM_SEC_PARAMS]

But it doesn't send anything. Any idea if I'm doing something wrong here ? Not much info or examples on the subject anywhere.

 

Update : I found what I was doing wrong. Updating it here so other people know.

After defining my custom properties in LaMa, I had to go on my Provider Definition and on the tab "Properties" and add the Secure Properties that I wanted to pass to my script. After that the LVM_SEC_PARAMS is going to my script with all the Secure Properties I selected in a single line.

entry.title: Re: How to decode STXL table without SE37 or SE38 transac...
entry.content:
Hi,

It is not possible to view the CLUSTD field directly as a table in SAP, because the STXL- CLUSTD field is a compressed data set and is stored in raw format in the database. SAP is designed to decipher this type of data only through specific business logic. However, there are several alternative ways to access this data and view its raw format.

Using the READ_TEXT function module, you can create a text in SAP table format and manipulate this table as you wish

entry.title: Re: Integration of pegging relationship between AATP and...
entry.content:
Hello,

The transferring of pegging result from SAP IBP to S4 or ECC should be a known solution by now based on the date of the post.

I had done the solution in SDI, but the architecture should be relevant for RTI as well.

In SAP IBP, in order to transfer the pegged result you have to run the job "Data Integration: Extraction of Order-Based Planning Result". The data gets stored in 5 tables. Check this link https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_INTEGRATED_BUSINESS_PLANNING/7cc6c35770994957ade465f87ac9c3d4/af0af4cee4964dd6924494ac061ecff2.html?q=pegging%2520result.

For these data to be transferred to S4, ECC or any ERP tool a certain set of configuration has to be done:

1. DP Agent has to be installed. Note: for integration through SDI, you can install 2 DP Agents in one server. One for Inbound eg. Abap Adaptor and another for Outbound eg. File Adapter.

2. Pegging results are always transferred through File Adapter and store the result in a .csv file. 5 files which are basically the tables mentioned in the link provided. Check Note: 2479514 - Sample .csv and .cfg files for IBP RM SDI integration

3. Now, you need to transfer those files using a shell script to S4, ECC or ERP system in custom tables. And also the shell script should delete the existing entries in those 5 files, so that for the next time it can re-populate the new pegging results in the .csv files. 

Well, now you have all the pegging results in custom table, you are good to make your own reports or customizations to carry out your business requirements. 

Note:

1. The only catch in the whole puzzle is installing 2 DP Agents in the server. This information was provided by SAP during our project, it is not documented in a simple way in sap help.

2. For RTI, I think one DP Agent should do the wonders.

 

Kind regards

Alok

entry.title: Datasphere Replication flow - Delta load of LO or SAPI extractor from ECC
entry.content:
Dear Datasphere Experts,

This pertains to the extraction of LO extractors (2LIS*) from ECC to a third-party target, such as AWS, using a "replication flow" facilitated by premium outbound connectivity.

According to SAP documentation, only an initial load is supported when the source object lacks a primary key for ODP artifacts.

Reference: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/c8a54ee704e94e15926551293243fd1d/2267a9f6a5724ec0ae5632db4f9f70e5.html

Here are my questions:

  1. What are the possible workarounds for this limitation, considering LO extractors (2LIS*) are widely used across organizations, and delta functionality is essential?
  2. Is there a plan in SAP's roadmap (I couldn’t locate one) to provide delta functionality for all standard and custom extractions without primary key?

This is a common requirement as It work perfectly in SAP BW, and I believe it warrants attention. I would appreciate your guidance.

BR,

Yatendra

entry.title: [SAP FSM] How to delete the company on the Windows Client
entry.content:
There are two possible methods to delete the company within the SAP Field Service Management (FSM) Windows Client:

Method 1: Through the Login Window

  1. Open the FSM Windows Client
  2. Login with your company credentials
  3. The next window after logged in, shows you the company to select and through the 3-dot menu you can delete the company directly from this interface

Method 2: Using File Explorer
If the above method is ineffective, you can manually delete the company through File Explorer. The procedure varies depending on the installation type:

For Installations from SAP For Me:

  1. Close the FSM Windows Client
  2. Open File Explorer using the shortcut Win+E
  3. Type %appdata% in the address bar and press Enter
  4. Navigate to SAP SE\SAP Field Service Management\Data
  5. Delete the contents inside the 'Data' folder
  6. Start the SAP FSM WinClient and log in

For Microsoft App Store (UWP) Installations:

  1. Close the FSM Windows Client
  2. Open File Explorer using the shortcut Win+E
  3. Type '%localappdata%\Packages' in the address bar and press Enter
  4. Locate the folder associated with the 'SAP Field Service Management' application
  5. Navigate to the 'LocalState' folder and delete its contents
  6. Start the FSM Windows Client and log in

By following these steps, you can effectively delete the company from the FSM Windows Client.

Can anyone help out?

Restrictions:

  1. I would like to prefer a non generic solution. So any solution NOT based on TYPE REF TO data.
  2. I am not interessted in any obscure function modules like REFRESH_SELECT_OPTIONS or any like that.

Here is my coding to test:

DATA time_period TYPE d.

SELECT-OPTIONS s_timper FOR time_period NO-EXTENSION.

CLASS class DEFINITION
  FINAL
  CREATE PRIVATE.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    TYPES period_range TYPE RANGE OF d.

    CLASS-METHODS by_reference
      IMPORTING
        period TYPE REF TO period_range.

    CLASS-METHODS by_value
      IMPORTING
        period TYPE period_range.

    CLASS-METHODS by_reference_like
      IMPORTING
        period LIKE REF TO s_timper[].
ENDCLASS.

CLASS class IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD by_reference.
    DATA(tmp) = period.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD by_reference_like.
    DATA(tmp) = period.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD by_value.
    DATA(tmp) = period.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  " Works!
  class=>by_value( period = s_timper[] ).
  
  " ERROR
  " type period = range of d with header line
  " type s_timper = range of d
  class=>by_reference( period = REF #( s_timper[] ) ).
  
  " Works
  class=>by_reference_like( period = REF #( s_timper[] ) ).

entry.title: Discrepancy between PO and SO items while adding / change in Quantities not reflecting in SO.
entry.content:
The Discrepancy between PO and SO during Creation and adding new line items / changing the quantities in the PO those are not reflecting while transferring to SO, using Output type: ZBSA in PO.

This issue occurs in the XXX (Production System), and the same behavior is observed in XXX (ECC6 Systems) and XXX (EHP8).

The discrepancies between the PO and SO are identical across all three systems.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Discrepancy between PO and SO items while adding / ch...
entry.content:
What you are seeing is correct - SOs will *not* dynamically update if changes are made to the PO created from the SO; 

In procurement / MM terms, once the Sales Order is "used up" to create the PO, the PO becomes the most important document (as it is in this part of the process the qty is changed). 

This has always been the system behaviour - the SO to PO process is only "one way" (creating the requirement, ordering, receiving and invoiced / paid for it), there is no "backwards" facing update to the SO once it's used. 

It's worth pointing out this expectation is not correct (SO is generated for X amount, PO is for that qty) - Invoicing, Billing and payments all go on what was in the initial order, and the PO - if over or under - will show a variance to check. 

If you want to add more items to the PO, you *must* generate a separate SO for the additional items, or increase the SO also (depending on the overall SD / SO process you are using, this can be used to update the PO, or the PO is manually increased by the same user creating the additional SO).

But in short, SOs and POs are not dynamically linked, they are fixed documents once generated (unless manually changed and additional SOs by the end user).

Thanks for the question!

entry.title: SAP EWM Podcast - Episode 17 - Batch management with SAP EWM!
entry.content:
Welcome to our blog post for the SAP EWM Podcast Episode 17 – Batch management with SAP EWM.

You can listen to the podcast episode in English and in German on Spotify, Amazon Music, Apple Podcasts or Sway! Enjoy!

Batch Management is relevant for every company! 

Almost all industries and sectors - and not only producing companies – have the requirement to manage and track homogeneous partial quantities of materials or articles across the entire supply chain. This can be a specific delivery quantity of an article in retail, a production batch in food production, a specific quantity of screws delivered by a supplier in discrete industry which are used as components for production or any kind of in chemistry or pharma industry. 

SAP EWM solves this requirement with the help of the SAP Batch, which is seamlessly integrated into all SAP EWM processes and enables seamless tracking of these partial quantities across the supply chain.

The SAP Batch - a short introduction

In an SAP ERP system, material quantities and stock values are usually managed on storage location level. For many reasons, it could be that inventory management at material level is not sufficient. For example if different production batches of a product need to be managed or if delivery lots or production lots from suppliers need to be managed clearly separated from each other in the system. Differentiating characteristics are, for example, the country of origin, the production date, the supplier batch, the best-before date and many more.

The batch is an SAP ERP master data!

And that’s where the SAP Batch comes into the game. In order to use batches for a material the Batch Management Requirement Indicator must be set in the SAP ERP material master. The batch management requirement indicator makes it mandatory for a material to capture a batch for each partial quantity of the material.

The batch itself is also an SAP ERP master data. It consists of a batch master record and an optional classification. The batch master record already contains many standard fields that are logistically relevant and can be used in EWM. The country of origin, the manufacturing and expiry date, the supplier batch and also the batch status are such standard fields of the batch master for example. The batch is part of the inventory management, both in SAP Inventory Management and in EWM and it is a so-called stock separation criteria. 

The SAP Batch

If these standard fields are not sufficient, a batch can also be classified. Classification means that any characteristics can be defined to further differentiate a batch and make it unique. In this way, any stock-dividing criteria can be included in a batch.

It is not necessary to always assign values to every characteristic of a batch. And as already mentioned, the classification is optional, and customers can also work only with the batch master record. This means that customers can use the batch to define basically any stock-dividing criteria in the SAP standard!

 The Levels of Batch Management or "How unique is batch?"

Each partial quantity of the material thus becomes uniquely identifiable in the ERP on storage location level and in SAP EWM on storage bin or handling unit level. The uniqueness of a batch in SAP ERP can be defined on different levels.

1) A batch can be unique at SAP client level for example that means the batch ID will only exist once in the whole technical SAP client.

2) Another possibility is the uniqueness on material level that means a batch ID can exist only once per material. However, different materials can have batches with the same batch ID.

3) And the third level of uniqueness is the plant level that means a batch for a material is only unique within a plant and batch IDs in different plants can be completely different objects.

The good news is that SAP EWM can work with all these levels of ERP batch management. However, the batch at the plant level has limitations and is usually not used when batches are newly implemented.

The role of batches in SAP EWM

Since the batch is an ERP master data object, an embedded EWM in S/4HANA accesses this object directly. In decentralized EWM deployments, the batches, the classification and changes of batches are distributed from SAP ERP to EWM. Up to release EWM 9.5, the Core Interface (CIF) was used for this master data distribution. For the decentralized EWM on S/4HANA systems the BATMAS Idoc and also the classification IDOCs in ERP are used because the data model of the batch and the classification in the decentralized EWM on S/4HANA is the same data model as in SAP ERP.

And of course, batch creation is also possible within SAP EWM for example during EWM inbound delivery processing or via the dedicated app "Create Batch". In this use case EWM sends the batch including its characteristics back to the SAP ERP system after creation or changes in EWM.

Batches in EWM inbound processing
In SAP EWM batches can be created either manually or as part of the goods receipt, if they are not already transferred from ERP for example in an advanced shipping notification. In the inbound delivery, batches can be created manually or also in the background - with internal numbering or by entering an external batch ID.

Batch creation in EWM inbound processing

 

Standard characteristics such as the country of origin, the expiry date or the supplier batch can be automatically transferred to the batch from the preceding documents.  Customer-specific characteristics can be automatically filled by BAdI implementations which can transfer these values into the batch in the background as well. In addition, a check of the minimum remaining shelf life in goods receipt can be activated also, for example to prevent the receiving of expired materials or of materials with too short a remaining life.

Storage of batch managed stock in the EWM warehouse
By configuration, customers can also define in EWM whether different batches are allowed within a Handling Unit or in a storage bin, or whether only one batch is allowed per Handling Unit or per storage bin. This of course affects the packing and putaway processes within EWM.

EWM also makes it possible to manage the available stock in a storage bin for a batch-managed material in a batch-neutral way and to store the batch information only in the physical stock. This gives the picker the freedom to choose one of the available batches in case of mixed batch stock in a storage bin without the need to search for a certain predefined batch. 

Examples of EWM physical stock for batch managed materials

Batches in EWM outbound processing
This leads us directly to the goods issue process and the stock removal strategies for batches. EWM can either receive a specific batch from the ERP in the outbound delivery for batch-managed materials, or it can receive only certain selection criteria or even no batch specification at all. The given information is then taken into account in the SAP EWM stock removal strategy. In this way, customers can control, for example, that only goods that meet certain minimum remaining shelf lives are picked and shipped for certain customers.

EWM stock removal strategies based on predefined batch characteristics

Immediately after picking, EWM then sends the picked batches back to the SAP ERP outbound delivery and therefore significantly earlier than other 3rd party WMS systems with the goods issue posting.

In addition batches are also supported in SAP EWM production logistics which enables a seamless traceability from the goods receipt of components through staging, consumption and goods receipt of finished goods. This traceability is also ensured across system boundaries for example in the interaction with an MES system as we also discussed in our podcast about SAP Digital Manufacturing Integration.

Batch Status Management
Another important aspect is batch status management. If problems arise related to a batch for example quality issues or similar, then customers sometimes have to block a batch immediately for any goods movements to prevent further deliveries to customers. And this is exactly what batch status management supports. The batch status is a characteristic of the batch, which can have two status values: unrestricted and restricted. 

Configuration of restricted goods movements in warehouse for batches which are not in free use

 And exactly in the moment the batch status is set to restricted in one system it is also set in all other connected systems accordingly also for example in decentralized EWM systems. And based on EWM configuration it is possible to control which goods movements are then still allowed for a batch within the warehouse. For example, picking could be still possible but goods issue postings should be prevented.

Customers therefore only need to set one status centrally, for example in the SAP ERP system, to prevent batches with issues from being delivered further by logistics in any EWM managed warehouse. 

Split Valuation - Use batches to manage stock with different stock value 
The batch in EWM is also used when customers use the split valuation of materials in SAP ERP since split valuation basically has the same requirement as normal batches.

There are partial quantities of a product in stock which have to be distinguished from other partial quantities, in this case because of their stock value. SAP ERP uses split valuation for this purpose. SAP EWM uses the batch for materials which are not batch managed to manage differently valuated stock – the batch id corresponds in this case to the ERP valuation type.

For materials which are already batch managed the valuation type is directly linked to the batch master record.

Segmentation - Use batches to manage stock with for different markets 
Another use case of more granular stock differentiation than just on material level is demand and supply segmentation in SAP ERP, which is particular used in the retail and fashion industry.

These processes again have the requirement to divide the total stock of a material into multiple partial quantities with different characteristics. These characteristics in the segmentation can be, for example, the distribution channels and/or the product quality. 

Example:
A fashion company wants to control the stock of a certain product in a way that partial quantities are reserved for certain distribution channels like online stores and in-stores. And the company wants to procure and distribute the quantities for the different channels. This can be achieved with segmentation in SAP ERP.
In the procurement and in inventory management so called stock segments are used and in the distribution process so called requirement segments.

A segment is the combination of characteristics that makes such a partial stock quantity unique, for example stock of a certain material for the e-commerce channel. Also the combination of several characteristics is possible for example quality A of a material for the e-commerce channel.

In SAP S/4HANA segmentation requires batch managed materials and for each stock segment a batch is defined which has the corresponding stock segment assigned as a characteristic. That means a batch represents the stock of a certain combination for example the quality A of a material for the e-commerce channel.

Stock segmentation with SAP EWM

In procurement, companies can then build up stock for certain segmentations. In EWM, the corresponding stock is built up with the batch representing the stock segment and in the EWM inbound delivery the stock segment is also visible at delivery item level.

And in the outbound process, the ERP can then send a requirement segment in the outbound delivery item, which must then be picked by EWM. That means EWM only selects stock for picking with the corresponding stock segment. If there is no specification of a requirement segment for an outbound delivery item, EWM will perform a batch determination based on the segmentation strategy stored in the material master.

Segmented products are of course also supported in other EWM processes such as returns processes and also in production logistics.

Internal warehouse processes with batch involvement 
Stock Consolidation 
EWM stock consolidation can consolidate stocks in the warehouse even at batch characteristic level in order to better utilize warehouse capacity. This may result in mixed-batch stocks in one bin, but the different stocks must be identical in one or more batch characteristics from a logistical point of view, such as the expiration date or almost identical for example the expiration date of all consolidated batches in one bin is within a specified time period.

Stock consolidation in SAP EWM based on batch characteristica

 Recurring QM Inspections 
SAP EWM also supports recurring inspections for batch managed materials. In this case, the batch has the next inspection date as a characteristic. A report in EWM periodically checks batches based on this characteristic and, if necessary, creates inspection lots for quality inspection and posts the stock to quality stock. A successful QM inspection then determines the next inspection date, updates the batch characteristics with the new date and releases the stock again.

Recurring inspection with SAP EWM

 

Documentary Batch
In summary, it is fair to say that the SAP batch in EWM enables more complex processes. If EWM customers do not want to introduce inventory-managed batches but still want to achieve a certain traceability, the documentary batch is an option. The documentary batch is not master data but it is captured in goods receipt and in the goods issue process when picking is confirmed. The documentary batch is then stored in the batch field of the inbound and outbound delivery item for documentation reasons on document item level. This enables logistics to do a lean where-used approach via the documentary batch ID. But the documentary batch does support classification, batch status management and so on.

Batch Traceability 

Full transparency is only possible with the inventory managed SAP Batch!

Batch Information Cockpit
Customers receive the full "batch where-used list" only with the inventory-managed SAP batch. All material movements of a batch within the SAP ERP system can be analyzed with the help of the Batch Information Cockpit and a complete traceability from goods receipt through production and shipping processes is possible at the push of a button.

SAP Global Batch Traceability (GBT) 
For customers who have more advanced requirements with regards to batch traceability, the SAP Global Batch Traceability solution – or SAP GBT - can be a very useful complementary solution. It allows integrate various SAP and non-SAP systems that collect batch information and it enables company-wide batch traceability. 

SAP EWM can also deliver information to SAP Global Batch Traceability directly which not only contains the batch but also load carrier information that means Handling Unit data. This enables SAP GBT to do a batch tracing at pallet or load carrier level and makes it possible in exceptional cases to only recall a few pallets instead of an entire production batch – a huge benefit.

SAP Global Batch Traceability provides special reports and visualizations for this purpose, which can be used to analyze and trace the use of batches along the value chain on an ad-hoc basis to identify in a case of a problem critical deliveries in seconds. The solution is often used by chemical, pharmaceutical and consumer goods companies, but there are also use-cases in discrete manufacturing or in the automotive sector.

SAP Global Batch Traceability uses SAP EWM Handling Unit information

 SAP Business Network Material Traceability

SAP Business Network Material Traceability is a solution that also re-uses batch information from S/4HANA for traceability. Compared to the batch information cockpit in ERP and SAP GBT, this solution focuses on the cross-company traceability of product batches across all stages of a supply chain - from the supplier of the supplier to the end customer. In other words - External Tracking. 
The solution also works with batch information from the S/4HANA system, which can also be created in EWM.

SAP Busines Network Material Traceability

 Conclusion

The SAP Batch is a very powerful object that can solve many requirements related to product and batch traceability. Therefore it should be considered in any SAP S/4HANA implementation because it is the answer of SAP when it comes to traceability of stock on more granular level than the material. At first glance, it could create a little more recording effort in the warehouse, since it has to be additionally recorded or scanned in many processes. But for the whole company however this information is very valuable and often even required or mandatory. SAP EWM offers many possibilities to keep the effort of batch recording in the warehouse as little as possible for example by just scanning EAN128 barcodes. And there for complex processes also with batch managed materials can be highly automated with SAP EWM.

Additional Information
Batch Management with SAP - Product Documentation
How-to-Guide for the SAP EWM - SAP GBT Integration
SAP Global Batch Traceability
SAP Business Network Material Traceability
SAP Batch Information Cockpit

Please visit our SAP EWM Podcast Landing Page with all Episodes, Blog Posts and Podcast Notes!

entry.title: Re: Finite Scheduling not working during production order.
entry.content:
Hello

Please follow the steps outlined in the following note to avoid error message CY831:

334455 - Message CY831 for availability check

In addition to that, the capacity availability check of an order always considers only the basic load on any work center.

If the capacities have not been confirmed / committed, the system shows that there is enough capacity available for the order. To evaluate the basic load, transaction CM07 with overall profile SAPSFC010 is the a better option.

Please consider the information provided on the following notes:

159337 - Capacity Check: Basic Load

160613 - Capacity check: Capacity availability check

You will find more information on SAP Help under Logistics -> Production Planning and Control -> Capacity  Planning -> Capacity Leveling -> Capacity Availability Check -> Determination of Basic Load.

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Work Center Determination for Packing During Outbound Process
entry.content:
Recently a friend ask me for help, because he was not being able to dynamicly determine the correct work center on his POSC for the packing step. Although it is pretty easy, it is not clear how to do and due to that many times consultant determine fixed storage bin in the Process-Oriented Storage Control node on the POSC configuration.

The goal of this blog is to present how to make it, I will explore both options, the fixed and the dynamic methods.

For those who still have never setup the Process-Oriented Storage Control (aka POSC), let me add some context to what is POSC.

The POSC is a functionality designed to handle complex warehouse processes that involve multiple sequential steps upon the processes execution. POSC allows you to define and execute these steps in a structured manner, ensuring goods are processed efficiently and in the correct order.

The main activities perform with POSC are:

  • For inbound process, define a series of warehouse activities, such as unloading, deconsolidation, quality inspection, repackaging, and putaway. 
  • For Outbound process, define a series of warehouse activities, such as picking, repacking, staging and loading. 
  • The process is configured in alignment with your warehouse layout and business requirements. Each step corresponds to a physical or logical location in the warehouse.
  • Based on predefined rules, POSC determines the route goods will take through the warehouse, including interim stops like quality inspection or repackaging stations.
  • The system creates automatically the required warehouse tasks for each process step and guides workers through the execution.
  • It is particularly useful in warehouses dealing with goods requiring additional checks or processing before they can be stored or shipped from the warehouse.

How to determine the POSC

 

In order to determine the POSC, the system provides two options:

  • BY Warehouse Process Type (aka WPT), in the WPT you have two options:
    • Set a Warehouse Order Creation Rule (aka WOCR), which contain the POSC
    • Set the POSC directly in the WPT

  • BY Warehouse Order Creation Rule

Particularly, I never assign anything to the WPT, because the determination using WOCR has more dynamism. I am not exploring WOCR area in this blog, but there a plenty of blogs explaining in details how WOCR works and its determination, I read a few blogs and the ones below are which suggest to take a look:

  • SAP EWM Part 17: Warehouse Order Creation Rule written by Manish Gupta 
  • SAP-EWM- WOCR determination based on Activity area written by Rohela Khan

After read all the documentation above, you will be able to determine the POSC for your process.

Business Scenario

In my example, the dynamic determination was required because the warehouse has one physical place to perform the repacking during the outbound process, but for one process the consolidation group is relevant and for the other it is not.

Assumptions

For the configuration I am using there are below some assumptions:

  1. Product master data is maintained with a Stock Removal Control Indicator
  2. The picking is for products and not for HU (it would work, but this is my business case)
  3. Pick-HU is relevant for this POSC
  4. Storage bin in the pack work center is created

Configuration Steps

1 - Setup the Work Center

The only difference between this work center for the one which already exist in my system is the Check for Consolidation Group, in my scenario there is no check, except for the storage type and storage sections, all the other parameters are identical to the template work center created by the BC Set, just copy it and make your changes.

IMG Path: SCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Work Center > Define Work Center

Make sure to create a optimization for the work center determination, it will be important for the master data definition. As you can see, this is a sequential definition of which data has to be analyzed in order to determine the work center for the process in the most effectively sequence. 

IMG Path: SCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Work Center > Optimize Work Center Determination in Goods Issue

2 - Setup the Process-Oriented Storage Control

IMG Path:SCM EWM > EWM > Cross-Process Settings > Warehouse Task > Define Process-Oriented Storage Control

Define the External Storage Process Step

In my example, I am using the standard External Step OB02 - Pack, in case it is used by another process, make a copy to assign to the Storage Process - Definition.

Define the Process-Oriented Storage Control

In this configuration you can define to use a fix storage type, storage section and storage bin for the packing work center, if you do not want to use as fixed, mark the checkbox Rules-Based. Also important to define which WPT you need to have the creation of WPT to move from pack station to the next destination.

Define the Storage Process

Define the name, description and direction of your storage process, in this example I am doing for outbound process, so my direction is Stock Removal. 

Define the storage process steps

For my business scenario, I only have three steps:

1 - Picking (OB01) - to create the task to remove the stock from its source

2 - Pack (OB02) - to perform the pack/repack of the product before stage

3 - Prepare for Loading/Staging in GI (OB03) - to carry the goods after repacked to the goods issue zone, this will nobe executed, as I am not creating the warehouse task.

In my example, the warehouse task for the step OB02 will be automatically created after you complete the picking to move to GI zone, this is determined by the checkbox  Automatic Creation of Subsequent Warehouse Task.

Once the configuration above is done, you have to assign the WOCR to your process as explained in the first part of this blog.

Master Data Definition

The steps are only required if you defined rule-based determination, if you setup fix storage type and storabe bin it is supposed to be already.

For the dynamic determination, there are two master data required, see below the transaction an the maintainenca.

Transaction: /SCWM/PACKSTDT - Work Center Determination in Goods Issue

In the first step of configuration, we need the determine the releance parameters during the determination, this is where it interfere in the process, in my case I am using only the activity area has parameter for the determination.

Transaction: /SCWM/TWORKST - Work Center: Define Master Data Attributes

Define the Storage Bin that must be determined for you work center  

Testing

Outbound Delivery 340676180

The final destination is the staging zone the handover area, this handover area is a specificity of the client, ignore this word, it is just a goods issue zone. 

Warehouse Order 

Look that the WOCR was determined, in my case for test purpose I assigned directly in the WPT and the storage process is defined in the WOCR. As I said, I prefer to use WOCR determination by Activity Area.

In the log of Wo creation, we can see how the system determine the destination bin, if confirms that it was due to storage control.

Confirming the picking warehouse task - step OB01 (Tcode - /SCWM/TO_CONF)

Note 1: I am confirming by the warehouse monitor, use the way you perfer.

Note 2: make sure to have a pick-HU assigned to the WO, otherwise the HU will not get warehouse order reference and the process step will no be assigned to HU.

Display WT for Outbound

After confirming the first WT, system does not generated a new WT, because the pack step is not complete.

Executing the repack (Tcode /SCWM/PACK)

In this example, I will pack the product, because I picking from the source a partial quantity and not the full HU. It can be done in the RF Enviroment or SAP GUI/Fiori. After completing the repack, the system will automatically generate the creation of the next warehouse task

Open the pack station for you warehouse request (outbound delivery), to display the stock moved to there.

You can see the pick-HU on the tree of the left side. To complete the step, mark the HU and click in the button Complete Process Step for HU.

Confirming the second warehouse task - step OB02

In my scenario, the WPT defined in the Process-Oriented Storage Control is set to confirm immediately, so the task was created and generated after I complete the step.

If I look at the warehouse tasks, I can see that my pick-HU is already moved to the handover area, which was originally my final destination.

Conclusion

What was presented in this blog is the methods you can apply in your system to determine tha pack station for the Outbound Process.

It could still have two steps, one for staging it to the door and another one to load the truck, the concept is the, although, different master data.

In my process it ends at this point, the loading is performed by 3PL (that's why my name is HANDOVER), once it is finished the PGI is posted automatically by TM integration.

For the ones who does not know, I hope it can help, any further comments or suggestion for blog, please put in the comments and I can try to provide it.

 

 

Kindly regrads,

Janderson Livio

entry.title: Required Roles for SAP BW consultant to Work on SAP ECC
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

As a SAP BW consultant, I need to understand what roles or authorizations are typically granted to a BW consultant in SAP ECC to facilitate tasks such as:

• Running or debugging programs (if needed)

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Packs: a Call to Action for SAP S/4HANA Customers
entry.content: Hi @VeeraRajachandrasekhar, you need to take into account that the commercial worlds of Business Suite and S/4HANA are completely separated. The end of use rights in S4 has no influence on support rules in the Business Suite world whatsoever, and vice versa. Other than that, I'm not in a position to comment on potential solution-specific issues.

entry.title: Re: Required Roles for SAP BW consultant to Work on SAP ECC
entry.content:
Hi Amir

In Support we request the authorizations, which are outlined in the KBA-s:

  • 3352612 Authorizations for support users in the BW-BCT and BC-BW* cases
  • 3351970 Authorizations for support users in the BW Warehouse Management Area

I this covers the majority of the actions that you listed.

Of course everything depends on how the authorization concept is done in the system and it might be needed to make further adjustments.

br

Roland

entry.title: Re: Filing Status sync from BSI
entry.content: I think the information you are looking for can be found in SAP note 2968880 - Filing status 00 is not valid on date MM/DD/YYYY - HRPAYUS_MESSAGES205.

entry.title: Re: Power Designer Generation Origin property in repository
entry.content:
Hi Alexandre,

you have to find your PDM table in the PMOBJT repository table. It has column called HIST, which contains foreign key into PMTEXT table into TXID column. Join it and you get the value of the History property, which contains information about the generation link (from your CDM object into your PDM object). But it is not that simple, because it contains string like this:

ORG {967ED8D1-61C6-4CD7-AE22-57B97A48C8C2}
DAT 1732291988

So you have to understand the structure of this property and decompose it properly. Btw that 36 chars long text is ObjectID of the entity you are looking for.

I have years of experience in customizing and extending PowerDesigner so in case you need help in any PD-related area, you can find me on LinkedIn and I can help you quickly solve issues like this.

Regards,

Ondrej Divis

entry.title: New Installation of SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 – Part 1 – Maintenance Planner
entry.content:
This procedure is Part 1 of the series which describes the very first steps to download the media. We will focus on creating Sandbox type instance with co-deployed (embedded) gateway.

Please find other blogs as follows at S/4HANA SQUARE ONE


                  SAP S/4HANA  SQUARE  ONE

GREENFIELD IMPLEMENTATION    PART1 - MPPART2 -  Installation
PART3 -  BP Activation
PART4 - Fiori ActivationPART5 - DATA MIGRATION
S/4HANA 2023fps1 fps0fps1 fps0fps1 fps0fps1 fps0 
S/4HANA 2022fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0 
S/4HANA 2021fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0 
S/4HANA 2020fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps0

As a prep you may like to empty the download basket for your S-000 user so that any files added to the download basket are meant for this task and will help to ensure that only the required files are included.

As a prep you may like to empty the download basket for your S-000 user so that any files added to the download basket are meant for this task and will help to ensure that only the required files are included.

 


You need to login to SAP Maintenance Planner to access system assigned to your S-user. Please ensure landscape data is uploaded to SAP support portal via LMDB of SAP Solution Manager. The diagram below shows the Maintenance planner cloud service and how it integrates with Customer system.

 

 


The source system is first defined in Maintenance planner (1) generates (2) stack.xml, used for (3) Installation and (4) Upgrade and then system is (5) registered with Solution Manager. The system data is synchronized with Landscape Management Database (LMDB). From LMDB the data is (6) uploaded to customer profile in SAP support portal. (7) Data Fetch occurs using Solman daily job – “Landscape Fetch”.

If there is issue in replicating you may be asked to apply Note 2186164 – Problem in replicating systems to SAP Maintenance Planner

TIP: This data based on Customer Number is used by Maintenance Planner for all planning activities. Also ensure you use same Customer Number to define system in sapnet. Use the S-user id of this Customer Number.

Login to SAP Maintenance Planner and select the SAP Fiori tile Plan for SAP S/4HANA

In the next screen,choose Install a new SAP S/4HANA system, Manual Installation. Here you can click on the link for the SAP S/4HANA on SAP Help Portal for the documentation and click on Next.

In the next screen enter SID = S4H; select Target Version = SAP S/4HANA 2023; Target Stack = 02 (10/2024) FPS
TIP: The component SAP Predictive integrator is selected by default but please note this component will not be installed by SWPM/SUM but has to be manually installed on the database.
Click on Next.
TIP: The selection of component above will only download the files. You have to install them as per the procedure for the component. 

In the next screen choose Co-deployed with Backend. Please refer SAP Fiori Deployment Options and Landscape recommendations for detailed information. Please select checkmark Target Product Instances as applicable depending on which SAP FontEnd Applications you need among UI for SAP S/4HANA, SAP Human Capital Management, SAP Master Data Governance and SAP Travel Management.

In the next scree click on Continue Planning

In the next screen Click on Next

In the next screen select OS/DB dependent files checkmark Linux on x86_64 bit and click on Confirm Selection.

You will get warning as below. Go through the Note and click on Continue.

In the next screen click on Next.

Click on NON-ABAP and you find that only DE and EN are selected by default. Select any other languages you require for installation.

TIP: The files are selected above for download only. Later on during installation, you have to select the languages in the SUM tool.

In the next screen click on Download Stack XML, Download Stack Text File, Download PDF and Export to Excel to download the files to your desktop. Out of these you will have to ftp the stack.xml file to the server. It will look something like this – MP_Stack_1002146477_20241122_.xml

TIP: Before you click on Push to Download Basket on next screen, please ensure that download basket is empty so that the download contains only the files needed for SAP S/4HANA installation.

Click on Push to Download Basket. This will load the Download Basket with files you need.

In the pdf file downloaded above, in the Planned Changes section there is a link provided that takes you directly to Service Marketplace to download the media.

Download all the media and separate the following into separate directories for convenience under /sapmedia/download. This includes files from Maintenance Planner 

You need the files below for the installation, please check if these are included in the maintenance planner download:

SWPM20SP19_1-80003424.SAR – Latest version of Software Provisioning Manager
SUM20SP21_5-80002456.SAR - Latest version of Software Update Manager

SAP HANA DATABASE:
HANA Database and Database Client media is already provided in the Maintenance Planner download basket.

Please refer SAP Note 3426239 - SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Package Stack 02: Additional Release Information for SAP HANA database release details. However, please check Note to ensure there are no problems with the SAP HANA release you select. Refer SAP Note 2655761 - SAP S/4HANA - restrictions and recommendations regarding specific revisions of SAP HANA database for use in SAP S/4HANA

IMDB_SERVER20_080_0-80002031.SAR - Latest version of SAP HANA Database as of this blog (Linux x86_64 64bit)

IMDB_CLIENT20_022_32-80002082.SAR -  SAP HANA Client (Linux x86_64 64bit)

If you want to install LiveCache you will require the SAP HANA database LCAPPS plugin from Software Downloads => SUPPORT PACKAGES AND PATCHES => SAP HANA PLATFORM EDIT 1.0 =>  HANA LCAPPS => SAP HANA LCAPPS 1.00 

TIP: If the above path is not available, please search for below file in the url https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/softwarecenter/support/index 
IMDB_LCAPPS_2080_0-20010426.SAR - Corresponding version of SAP HANA LCAPPS (Linux x86_64 64bit)

Download all the media and separate the DB files IMDB* into separate directories for convenience under /sapmedia

OTHER FILES:
In addition, please download file/s for best practices content activation from SAP Note 34455517 - SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Cloud, private edition FP-Stack 02 (10/2024) content activation note. The file will be in this format BPS4CONT202302_0_0-80008741.ZIP

crlbag.p7s from https://tcs.mysap.com/crl/crlbag.p7s

COMPONENT FOR INCIDENTS:

You can open OSS messages using component BC-UPG-MP

OTHER REFERENCES:

Maintenance Planner - User Guide
Maintenance Planner Help Portal
Maintenance Planner – Frequently Asked Questions

Mahesh Sardesai
Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Regional Implementation Group

entry.title: Parsing multipart/mixed-form data format in BTP CI
entry.content:
Dear Community,

BTP CI receives multipart/mixed-form data from OData V4 API, which requires parsing the message in case of an error. Error description appears in the multipart data response, technically  OData API call was successful,it says HTTP 200 ok.
Based upon data content received from OData, BTP CI derived whether a call has been processed successfully or not.

For that BTP CI is required to parse this message. and it is expected to read JSON structure of the error description and HTTP content text ( HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found) in the example of format,  how this will be achieved through BTP CI?

Example of Multipart/mixed data:

 --789901289D3B067293B4A5D8BC2033B80
        Content-Type: application/http
        Content-Length: 1279
        content-transfer-encoding: binary
        
        HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found
        Content-Type: application/json;odata.metadata=minimal;charset=utf-8
        Content-Length: 1097
        
        {
        "error": {
            "code": "/SCWM/ODATA_API/126",
            "message": "task item 1 doesn't exist in warehouse1.",
            "target": "$Parameter/_it",
            "@SAP__core.ContentID": "1",
            "details": {
                "message": "task 1 doesn't exist in warehouse."
            }
        }
    }

--789901289D3B067293B4A5D8BC2033B80--


Thank you in advance.

Yours sincerely,

Sateesh

entry.title: New Machine Learning features in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08
entry.content:
With the SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 release, several new features have been released in the SAP HANA Cloud Predictive Analysis Library (PAL), an enhancement summary is available in the What’s new document for Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) enhancements overview with SAP HANA Platform 2.0 SPS 08.

The feature highlights for the current release are described in more detail below

Classification and regression enhancements

A new multi-task multilayer perceptron (MLP) function is introduced, enabling multi-label classification or multi-target regression scenarios. Using a multi-task learning neural network, a single ML model allows to predict multiple, related target columns at once, as the model captures both common features across tasks as well as task-specific information with the same prediction model.

  • It leverages the commonalities between related tasks to improve the performance, generalization, and training efficiency, and moreover enables efficient use of data, better feature extraction, knowledge transfer, regularization, and end-to-end learning.
  • Furthermore, the function supports early stopping using validation data to avoid overfitting.
  • Users of the new multi-task MLP also benefit from shorter MLP training times and models with improved accuracy.

     The new function provides unique prediction model capabilities for example scenarios like

  • automated multi-field value proposals or pre-filling of forms (e.g. Sales Order Automation)
  • or predicting multiple price-/sales-targets (average, minimum, etc.) in a single model.

  • For a more detailed introduction to the new algorithm see the following blog post Advancing to Multi-task Multilayer Perceptron: a new Neural Network design in HANA Machine Learning  https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/advancing-to-multi-task-multilayer-perceptron-a-new-neural-network-design/ba-p/13730435

 

Unified regression and -classification now support permutation feature importance,

  • a new and trending method in global explainability to evaluate the contribution of individual features to the overall predictive power of a model.
  • This is achieved by measuring the decrease of a model’s performance when a feature‘s values are being shuffled around. A detailed explanation and examples are also given in this blog Global Explanation Capabilities in SAP HANA Machine Learning.

                  Classic feature importance vs permutation feature importance reports 

 

The unified regression function is enhanced with prediction- and confidence interval

  • Interval estimates and the point estimate of response values in the unified prediction or scoring regression procedure. The prediction interval predicts distribution of individual future observation of the target, while the confidence interval predicts the mean statistic of the future target population.
  • Now supported with Generalised Linear Models (GLM), Multiple Linear Regression (MLR), Random Decision Trees (RDT), and Hybrid Gradient Boosting Tree (HGBT).
  • Complementing this, a new interval quality function is added, which aids to evaluate the quality of an interval

 

A new outlier detection for regression function is added,

  • providing the ability to detect point outliers in data used when training linear or tree-based regression models using MLR and HGBT regressors.
  • Outliers in training data get identified based on residual analysis and outlier-score evaluation, then can be excluded for model training and allowing to build improved regression model predictions.

 

A new FairML classification and regression function is added

  • New function aiding to mitigate “bias” and potential “unfairness” in ML model predictions related to affected groups of individuals
  • Binary classification or regression models based on Hybrid Gradient Boosting Tree (HGBT) are supported
  • Fairness evaluation is aided with metrics like demographic parity, equalized odds, and more
  • For a detailed use case and function example see the following blog https://blogs.sap.com/2023/12/08/fairness-in-machine-learning-a-new-feature-in-sap-hana-pal/

 

The Hybrid Gradient Boosting Tree (HGBT) function has been improved

  • New objective function Huber Loss and reweighted square for more robust regression models
  • Improved classification models by support of F1, recall, precision as cross validation metrics and weight scaling of target values to address imbalanced classes or weight scale values related to different costs
  • For improved early stopping during model optimization, the validation metric for early stopping can now be explicitly set.

Further regression and classification enhancements included

  • Improved KNN search, by matrix-enabled search method
  • Option to limit results to top N classes in multi-class predictions with unified classification
  • Enhanced one-hot encoding of categorial features, supports aggregating infrequent values, into a single output for each feature (SVM, MLP, MLinR, MCLogR)
  • Data-parallel / massive execution support with Multiple Linear-, Exponential-, Bi-Variate Natural Logarithmic-, Bi-Variate Geometric-, Polynomial-Regression

 

Time series analysis and forecasting enhancements

New outlier detection function in time series analysis

  • Automatic or manual time series residual analysis, incl. intermittent time series detection, and missing value handling, auto smoothing of time series
  • Automatic selection of the outlier detection algorithm from the residual series incl. Z1, Z2, Inter-Quartile-Range, Mean absolute deviation, Isolation Forest and DBSCAN
  • Support of a voting ensemble across multiple outlier scoring metrics

 

Improved seasonality- and trend decomposition in time series analysis

  • Seasonal and trend decomposition using the STL-method (season, trend, low-pass window), based on LOESS (locally estimated scatterplot smoothing)
    and a super smoother function
  • Allowing for seasonality tests with more robust decomposition and less sensitive to outliers

Time series analysis permutation-based external feature importance evaluation

  • a new and trending method in global explainability to evaluate the contribution of individual features to the overall forecast model accuracy.
  • This is achieved by measuring the decrease of a model’s performance when a feature‘s values are being shuffled around. A detailed explanation and examples are also given in this blog Global Explanation Capabilities in SAP HANA Machine Learning.
  • Supported with the following time series algorithms ArimaX, Bayesian Structural Time Series (BSTS), Long term time series (LTSF), Additive model time series analysis (AMTSA)

Forecast prediction interval evaluation

  • a new interval quality function is added, which aids to evaluate the quality the forecasted prediction interval

Long term time series (LTSF) enhancements

  • New Neural Network model types (NLinear, DLinear, XLinear) for improved speed and forecast accuracy
  • Rigorous decomposition support of neural network time series models into TREND, SEASONAL and EXOGENOUS_CONTRIBUTIONS parts

 

New data-parallel, massive execution time series function support

  • Online BCPD, Forecast Accuracy Measure, Time series outlier detection, Single Exponential Smoothing, Double Exponential Smoothing, Triple Exponential Smoothing, Brown Exponential Smoothing, Croston's Method, Linear Regression with Damped Trend and Seasonal Adjust, White Noise Test, Seasonality Test, Trend Test, Change-Point Detection, Intermittent Time Series Forecast (ITSF)
  • Allowing segmented time series analysis and forecasting and fastest result when modelling thousands of time series in parallel, identified by a segmentation,  grouping column

 

 

AutoML and ML pipelines enhancements

General AutoML configuration and optimization improvements

  • Optimized pipeline operator selection, considering operator sequence and importance using operator connection constraints
  • Finetuning phase of best pipeline after initial AutoML optimization, by further evaluation the remaining parameter combination options and without changing the selected pipeline operators itself
  • Support of random search optimization for small AutoML configurations and faster results, for example with simple time series scenarios
  • Faster AutoML pipeline predictions interpretability based on a more light-weight shapley explainer model (SHAPGlobal surrogate). More details can be found in the following blog post Demystifying Pipeline Explanation for Time Series Data.
  • AutoML scenario configuration verification option
  • Pipeline predictions supporting more algorithm specific parameters
  • Score function for hold-out sample based classification / regression model evaluation
  • Improved AutoML progress logging in Python ML client, with full log information

AutoML Time Series enhancements

  • Support for auto exponential smoothing, polynomial feature generation,
    HGBT-/ MLinR- regression operators for time series
  • SPEC as a new evaluation metric supported

New data-parallel AutoML and ML Pipeline modeling functions allow for developing and executing multiple models in parallel by

  • building AutoML classification, regression or time series models in parallel on data subsets, identified by a grouping column,
  • using the new massive AutoML and Pipeline SQL procedures

This allows for fastest parallel AutoML modeling, like e.g. segmented AutoML time series. At the same time SAP HANA Cloud’s database workload management is best applied to control and limit compute resource consumed.

 

 

Further AI function enhancements

New Quantile Transform data preprocessing

  • The Quantile Transform automatically applies a non-linear transform, that maps numeric input columns of varying data distributions, into a uniform or normal target distribution making it more suitable as an input for many ML models.
  • Preserves the rank order of the data, and reduces the impact of outliers, makes data less sensitive to extreme values

New financial data analysis functions

  • Benford Analysis, trending algorithm used to detect anomalies in financial data
    • One of the (not so) well-known statistical observations is the fact that in many datasets the leading significant digits are not equally distributed. If all digits were represented equally, then they would appear 11.1 percent (1/9TH) of the time. However, when analyzing real-world datasets, it is revealed that the distribution of the leading digits follows the Bedford’s law, also known as the first-digit law.
    • It is now very easy to validate if a dataset obeys Bedford’s law or not. A first step means, very commonly applied in financial applications is to detect unexpected value distributions and e.g. potential fraudulent transaction data.
  • Hull-White Model, trending used in modeling the time evolution of interest rates, rates, which are required for price estimation of financial instruments based on interest rate derivatives.
    • To apply the Hull-White model it first needs to be adopted to match existing market conditions (interest rates). This is achieved  by providing the values of the drift term of the Hull-White model as a time series as input table. The simulation will then provide the mean value for a given number of simulation paths (also specified as an input parameter), their variance, as well as the upper and lower bounds.

New MLP Recommender function

  • Powerful neural network function to predict binary targets such as click-through rates, a fundamental part in recommender systems.
  • The MLP recommender function does not require specific user and item details, and can include extra data features, unrelated features without any user connection.
  • The algorithm employs a dual-stream MLP framework, capable of handling larger-scale and “complicated” input data, designed to boost the recommendation performance.

Text processing enhancements

  • New RDT model-based text classification
  • Improved TF-IDF providing control over stopword usage

Improved Clustering

  • Pre-calculated distance matrices supported with agglomerate hierarchical- and spectral clustering with the unified clustering function
  • Improved one-hot encoding support with spectral clustering

Data-parallel / massive execution support

  • Isolation Forest, Variance Test, Inter-Quartile Range, Fast Fourier Transform, K-Optimal Rule Discovery (KORD), K-Means, FP-Growth

 

Automated Predictive Library

Automated Predictive Library (APL) 2425 release enhancements

  • Reduced execution time around 15% for binary classification or regression models (gradient boosting), and around 35% for multi-class classification.
  • Better estimates and faster calculation of variable interactions
     See what's New 2311
  • Local explanations for time series See what's New 2403

 

Python machine learning client enhancements

The full list of new methods and enhancements within the hana_ml releases 2.17-2.22 are summarized in the changelog for hana-ml as part of the documentation.

Selected key enhancements include

AutoML and pipeline modeling improvements

  • Visual editor support for the AutoML scenario configuration

 

  • Massive, data parallel AutoML support 

  • PipelineProgressMonitor python runtime_platform specification for jupyter, vscode, bas, console
  • AutoML optimization enhancement with connection constraints and connection visualizations

     

More detailed references to the described enhancements can also be found in the New Machine Learning features in SAP HANA Cloud - SAP Community blog posts.

Furthermore you can find example notebooks illustrating the highlighted feature enhancements under hana-ml-samples/Python-API/pal/notebooks at main · SAP-samples/hana-ml-samples · GitHub, like for example H2SPS08_AutoML.ipynb, H2SPS08_RegressionClassification.ipynb, H2SPS08_TimeSeries.ipynb, H2SPS08_MiscEnhancements.ipynb and H2SPS08_Design-time code generation.ipynb.

Explore and enjoy all the new capabilities!
